unassigned_stints.reason: No positive dimension links this stint to the biography. The biography is a New Zealand lawyer ('barrister and solr., 1883'; 'judge, N.Z. sup. obl., 1921') with no Canadian event; the stint is a 'Private Secretary' in Canada in 1877-1878, when the bio subject (b. 1861) was ~16 and per the entry still being educated at 'pub. schls. and Otago Univ., N.Z.'. Colony, tenure_overlap, position_sim, honour, edition_cooccurrence, and place_inherited checks all FAIL; editions do not overlap (1877-78 vs 1922-33). The only agreement is the single initial 'S' matching the second forename of a surname shared by 96 officials, which is far below the bar for a merge.

notes: One-bio, one-stint case with no plausible link. A 16-year-old serving as a Private Secretary in Canada while attending Otago University in New Zealand is effectively impossible; the stint almost certainly belongs to a different S. Adams not present in this dossier. Recommend the stint be re-blocked against other Adams biographies or left unlinked.
